hero.tagline: 5+ years owning IT project delivery & pre-sales
hero.subtagline: PMP · Cross-functional leadership · Risk & stakeholder management

about.title: About
about.summary: Project manager with 5+ years in IT infrastructure delivery and pre-sales. Led 6+ cross-functional projects end-to-end, closed ¥1.3M+ in contracts. PMP-certified, fluent in ITIL 4, and a credible bridge between technical teams and business stakeholders. Track record of 0-to-1 platform builds, multi-region multi-team coordination, and disciplined risk and change management — backed by a strong technical foundation (HCIE-Cloud + CKA + CISP) so I can weigh in credibly on technical decisions.

categories.name: Process & Governance
items: SLA design, risk assessment, quality control

categories.name: Requirements & Solutions
items: Axure high-fidelity prototypes, UML, data flow

categories.name: Technical Literacy (PM Lens)
items: Cloud architecture (HCIE-Cloud) — works fluidly with infra teams

highlights: Spearheaded the APAC POS terminal OS migration (see case studies): led 6 in-house engineers + regional implementers; ran weekly reviews, risk boards, and stage-gate sign-offs; coordinated across 13 APAC markets; delivered with zero business disruption

Led the Workspace ONE MDM China localization (see case studies): managed 6 in-house + 5 vendor engineers with a clear RACI; aligned Legal, InfoSec, and Infrastructure on a PIPL-compliant solution and SLA standards

Owned the infrastructure reliability program for 300+ stores and 2,000+ endpoints: built a regional O&M SOP, ran 20 training sessions, shipped 50+ technical documents; lifted regional first-line self-recovery from 30% to 75% and cut MTTR 40%

Championed the 5,000+ endpoint cloud imaging rollout: coordinated across Infrastructure, Application, Business, and Regional functions; the standardized imaging system cut labor cost 50% and enabled 13-country rapid deployment

items.company: Shanghai Huaxun Network System Co., Ltd.
highlights: Owned pre-sales and solution design for 10+ enterprise customers — designed the SIPG (2,000+ nodes) ITIL 4 hybrid-cloud O&M platform, ran 12 requirements workshops surfacing 30+ functional points, delivered the SRS and 5 standardized solution templates
Closed pre-sales wins: drove the full pre-sales cycle on the McDonald's network monitoring project, closing a ¥300K contract; ran 5 POCs (Yinzhou Bank, Shanghai FSA) at 100% technical acceptance
Led end-to-end delivery for finance and retail customers — Pufa Credit Card, Starbucks AMS, JD's 2,000+ endpoint desktop cloud; resolved 50+ on-site incidents with high CSAT
Owned key-account program management: full-lifecycle requirements for Dun & Bradstreet Data Blocks; designed JD's 2,000+ endpoint desktop-cloud solution

items.role: IT System O&M Engineer / Project Lead (Intern → FTE)
highlights: Owned the enterprise security program: coordinated security controls across 2,000+ endpoints and 300+ servers; led the IAM zero-trust project to production; designed ITIL-based Jira incident/change workflows that materially raised process standardization

Ran a portfolio of 3 concurrent large-scale migrations — Confluence cross-DC (200 → 2,000 users), Active Directory (2,000+ accounts), full CDN migration (30% latency reduction) — zero-incident delivery across the lot
Owned the digital dashboard data API project: gathered business requirements, defined the KPI system, built the APIs; managed full lifecycle of OKR and enterprise cloud drive systems

items.name: Decathlon China Android POS Hardware Innovation
items.background: Replaced the existing Windows POS hardware (high power draw, limited extensibility, unsustainable O&M cost) with a lightweight Android POS platform and a new in-store hardware standard.
highlights: Scope: decomposed into 4 work packages (hardware selection / industrial design / O&M / RFID subsystem) with clear milestones and stage-gate deliverables
Stakeholder mgmt: coordinated local suppliers, business, and regional implementation teams; ran weekly reviews and joint design reviews
Risk mgmt: built a three-tier risk register for business continuity, hardware supply, and compliance; 100% closure on critical risks
Delivery: 40% hardware CapEx reduction (~¥2M+), 99.8% RFID identification success rate, predictive terminal-failure alerting
items.result: Finalized and piloted the new-generation Android POS hardware: 40% hardware CapEx reduction, 99.8% RFID identification success rate, 70% faster bulk-item read throughput, and predictive terminal-failure alerting — completing the APAC POS hardware standardization ecosystem

items.name: Decathlon APAC POS Terminal OS Migration
items.background: Migrated 2,000+ POS terminals from CentOS 7 to Windows 10 IoT across 13 APAC markets ahead of the CentOS 7 EOL — without a single business disruption.
highlights: Scope: decomposed into 4 work packages (imaging / cross-cloud sync / automation / O&M)
Schedule: stood up a central dispatch, orchestrated regional concurrency, and completed 2,000+ terminals within 7 months
Risk: phased migration strategy avoided business peaks and compliance risk — zero business disruption
Communication: coordinated 13-country stakeholders via weekly reviews, risk boards, and stage-gate sign-offs
Quality: 99.95% success rate, 1,500+ person-hours saved
items.result: Migrated 2,000+ POS terminals with zero business disruption, 99.95% success rate, saving 1,500+ person-hours

items.name: Workspace ONE MDM China Localization
items.background: Localized the French SaaS Workspace ONE endpoint management platform to comply with China PIPL.
highlights: Team mgmt: led 6 in-house + 5 vendor engineers with clear RACI and weekly delivery cadence
Stakeholder mgmt: aligned Legal, InfoSec, and Infrastructure on PIPL-compliant solution and SLA standards
Architecture decision: ran SWOT across public cloud vs. on-prem DC; selected bare-metal + VCenter cluster
Change mgmt: phased migration delivered 100% endpoint transition with zero disruption
items.result: 100% endpoint migration with zero disruption, built a PIPL-compliant endpoint management system

items.name: SIPG IT Unified O&M Management Platform
items.background: Designed an ITIL 4-aligned hybrid-cloud O&M platform for Shanghai International Port Group, consolidating fragmented toolchains.
highlights: Requirements: ran 12 workshops, surfaced 30+ functional points, delivered the SRS
Architecture: designed a unified portal integrating Zabbix + Ansible + ServiceNow
Commercial: led POC validation and 5-round technical defense; resolved client data-security and performance concerns
items.result: Closed Phase-1 ¥1M contract, laid groundwork for Phase-2 ¥3M expansion

items.name: AsiaInfo Contractor Digital Management Platform
items.background: Replaced manual contractor management (200+ headcount, error-prone, no workflow closure) with a digital platform covering the full contractor lifecycle — delivered as a 0-to-1 build.
highlights: Requirements: interviewed regional assistants and HR; mapped 8 core processes (on/offboarding, attendance, payroll)
Solution design: defined three modules (employee records / approval workflow / data dashboard); integrated OA for end-to-end automation
Change mgmt: ran 10+ regional training sessions, stood up a tiered issue-response mechanism
Delivery: 96% faster on/offboarding, 50% reduction in HR time, zero payroll errors
items.result: End-to-end digitalization of contractor management — 96% faster on/offboarding, 50% reduction in HR time, zero payroll errors; reusable methodology and platform assets rolled out across AsiaInfo regional HR
